extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won a technical uphill finish after the peloton kept the break on a tight 90-second leash throughout.
tactical_patterns: Six teams collaboratively policing break at 90-120 seconds reveals coordinated sprint stage control between rival leadout trains.
VAN DER SANDE Tosh launched premature uphill attack inside 1km, exposing sprinters' teams inability to control steep gradient finishes.
BENNETT positioned third wheel before gradient steepened, demonstrating positioning patience superior to traditional leadout dependency.
Break initially gained 2min on technical descending/winding roads where peloton cornerning advantage negated, then caught on climb.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: won without structured leadout on uphill drag, positioned himself third wheel then accelerated independently
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: notable support work for ROGLIČ throughout stage, commentators highlighted consistent performance quality
rider_signals.GRMAY Tsgabu: neutral: became father overnight, motivated but no tactical significance shown
rider_signals.VAN DER SANDE Tosh: neutral: aggressive late attack on uphill finish gained gap but third place shows mistimed effort
rider_signals.MEZGEC Luka: negative: leadout preparation negated by crash inside final kilometer despite time protection
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: involved in late crash but inside 3km protection, no time loss
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: neutral: crashed at finish but crossed line uninjured, retained white jersey
